armies.name: Raynor's Raiders
armies.playstyle: Flexible infantry and fire support. Marines hold lines; Marauders crack armored targets; Medics keep squads in the fight.

armies.name: Kerrigan's Swarm
armies.playstyle: Overwhelming numbers and reactive reinforcements. Zerglings flood objectives; Roaches hold; Kerrigan and the Queen anchor the swarm.

armies.name: Artanis Host
armies.tagline: Protoss Founders Edition — solo player box
armies.playstyle: Fewer models, higher impact. Zealots and Adepts trade efficiently; Stalkers and Sentries control the mid-board.

matchSteps.title: Choose Factions & Armies
matchSteps.summary: Each player picks a faction and army list.
matchSteps.body: Select Terran, Zerg, or Protoss and the sub-faction army each commander is fielding. The 2-Player Founders Edition matchup is Raynor's Raiders vs Kerrigan's Swarm.
tips: Use Starter Matchup for the box set armies.
Protoss Founders Edition is a one-player expansion — pair it with a second army for full games.

matchSteps.title: Pick Battle Format
matchSteps.summary: Skirmish or standard minerals; table size.
matchSteps.body: Agree on game size. Skirmish uses 1,000 minerals on a 36"×36" table (~1 hour). Standard uses 2,000 minerals on a 54"×36" table (~2 hours). Vespene for tactical cards is about 10% of your mineral budget.
tips: First game? Skirmish on the Founders play mat is ideal.
Tactical cards are paid with vespene — not minerals.

matchSteps.title: Mission & Deployment Draft
matchSteps.summary: Draft mission and deployment cards before play.
matchSteps.body: Use the physical card draft or Archon's Command Center to veto and select Mission and Deployment cards. Note the mission name and victory conditions — you will score during the Scoring phase each round.
tips: Command Center can generate custom maps — link in Official Resources.
No models start on the table — your army begins in reserves.

matchSteps.title: Confirm Army Lists
matchSteps.summary: Minerals on units; vespene on tactical cards.
matchSteps.body: Build lists within your mineral budget. Spend vespene only on faction and tactical cards. Starter box players can use the included rosters as written.
tips: Supply on the table is limited — losses free pool space for reserves.
Unit cards in the box are the source of truth for stats.

matchSteps.title: Set Up the Battlefield
matchSteps.summary: Terrain, objectives, empty table, supply pools.
matchSteps.body: Place terrain and assign height tiers. Mark mission objectives. Confirm both armies are in reserves — the table starts empty except terrain and entry points. Note each player's starting supply pool from the mission.
tips: Objective control uses total Supply within 3" — not model count.
Lost Temple terrain ships with the Founders Edition.

matchSteps.title: Roll for First Activation
matchSteps.summary: Winner chooses who activates first in Movement.
matchSteps.body: Roll off. The winner chooses who takes the first unit activation in Round 1 Movement. Passing during a phase claims the First Player Marker for the next phase.
tips: Sometimes passing in Movement lets you strike first in Assault.
First Player Marker holder picks who goes first in the next phase.

matchSteps.title: Fight the Battle
matchSteps.summary: Five rounds — Movement, Assault, Combat, Scoring.
matchSteps.body: Play five battle rounds. Each round: Movement (deploy from reserves), Assault (shoot and charge), Combat (melee), then Scoring. Players alternate activating one unit at a time within each phase until both pass or finish.
tips: Track supply pool — it increases each round.
Reinforce from reserves when losses free supply space.
